events.summary: Act 1: 10 cards vs The Guardian
Act 1, Boss: The Guardian, Deck: 10 cards

events.reasoning:
ACT 1 ROUTE PLANNING:

Boss: The Guardian - Key considerations: Guardian alternates between attack and defensive mode. Need to manage Mode Shift (deals damage when taking enough hits). Need steady damage output, not huge burst.

Map analysis - starting from Floor 1:
Option A: x=0 path: F1 Monster → F2 Monster → F3 Monster → F4 Monster(x=1) → F5 Monster(x=0) → F6 Rest(x=1) → F7 Unknown(x=2) → F8 Monster(x=1 or x=2) → F9 Treasure(x=2) → F10 Rest(x=1) → ... lots of fights, good for cards but risky HP

Option B: x=2 path: F1 Monster → F2 Unknown → F3 options... 

Option C: x=5 path: F1 Monster → F2 Monster(x=4) → F3 Unknown(x=5) → F4 Shop(x=5) → F5 Unknown/Monster(x=5 or x=6) → F6 Rest(x=5) or Rest(x=6)

Let me trace more carefully using the arrows:

F1: Monster(x=0)→[0] means edges to F2 node 0
F1: Monster(x=2)→[2] means edges to F2 node 2
F1: Monster(x=5)→[4] means edges to F2 node... wait, the arrows show which F2 nodes connect.

Let me read this differently. The number after → is the node index at the next floor.

Actually looking at the format: Monster(x=0)→[0,1] means from this node you can go to nodes indexed 0 or 1 on the next floor.

F1 x=0 → F2 nodes [0] → F2 Monster(x=0)
F1 x=2 → F2 nodes [2] → F2 Unknown(x=2)  
F1 x=5 → F2 nodes [4] → F2... wait indices dont match x values.

Let me just trace the paths. The <<<  marks available starting nodes.

Starting x=0: F1 Mon → F2 Mon(x=0) → F3 Mon(x=0) → F4 Mon(x=1) → F5 Mon(x=0) or Unk(x=2) → F6 Rest → good path, lots of fights for cards

Starting x=2: F1 Mon → F2 Unk(x=2) → F3 options → could reach shop or more unknowns

Starting x=5: F1 Mon → F2 Mon(x=4) → F3 Unk(x=5) → F4 Shop(x=5) → F5 Mon(x=6) or Unk(x=5) → F6 Rest(x=5) or Unk(x=6)

My priorities: Get cards early (fights), hit a rest site before boss, ideally get 1 elite for relic.

The left path (x=0) has many monsters which gives card rewards. Hits rest at F6. Then can continue through more fights.

The right path (x=5) has a shop at F4 which is early for spending gold.

I think the LEFT path (x=0) is best - maximum monster fights early means maximum card rewards to build the deck. Violet Lotus wants stance-change cards, so I need to pick up Crescendo, Tranquility, Empty Fist, etc.

ROUTE PLAN: Start x=0 (left), fight monsters, aim for rest at F6, then try to path through a treasure room and another rest before boss. Will try to hit 1 elite if HP allows.

Enemies: Louse, Louse. Hand: Defend, Eruption, Strike, Strike, Strike

events.reasoning:
FIGHT STRATEGY - 2 Louses:
Louse 0: 12 HP, intent 5 damage, Curl Up 3
Louse 1: 17 HP, intent DEBUG (unknown), Curl Up 3

Both have Curl Up 3 - first attack triggers 3 block. Low threat fight.

No stance cards available except Eruption. But I have no way to EXIT Wrath this turn (no Calm/Vigilance in hand). So I should NOT play Eruption - I would end turn in Wrath and take double damage.

TURN 1 PLAN:
- 3 energy available
- Play 3 Strikes at Louse 0: 3 x 6 = 18 damage, minus 3 Curl Up = 15 net. Louse 0 has 12 HP, so 15 > 12 = KILL.
- That leaves 0 energy, Defend unused.
- Incoming from Louse 1 only (Louse 0 dead). Intent unclear but likely 5-7 damage.
- Take 5-7 damage with no block.

Wait - should I Defend instead? Let me check:
- Defend + 2 Strikes at Louse 0: 5 block, 12 damage - 3 curl up = 9 net. Louse 0 lives with 3 HP.
- Then take damage from both louses (~10 total), block 5 = take 5.
- Next turn finish Louse 0, deal with Louse 1.

OR: 3 Strikes at Louse 0 kills it. Take ~5-7 from Louse 1. Net damage ~5-7.
VS: 2 Strikes + Defend. Block 5, take ~10 incoming, net ~5 damage. Louse 0 lives.

Killing Louse 0 this turn is better - it removes an attacker permanently. 
3 Strikes: 6+6+6 = 18, minus 3 Curl Up = 15 damage dealt. 15 > 12. Kill confirmed.

EXECUTE: Strike Louse 0, Strike Louse 0, Strike Louse 0.

Collect Weak Potion - useful for reducing damage in tough fights
View card reward options

Card reward analysis:

1. Inner Peace (1E, uncommon): In Calm: draw 3 cards. Otherwise: enter Calm. 
   - This is AMAZING with Violet Lotus. Enter Calm = safe stance. In Calm, draw 3 = find key cards.
   - Provides a Wrath exit AND draw engine. Core Watcher card.

2. Sands of Time (4E, uncommon attack): I think this is a Retain card that deals damage and reduces cost each turn. 4E is very expensive initially.
   - Not great early game when we need reliable damage/defense.

3. Mental Fortress (1E, uncommon power): I believe this gives block whenever you change stances (4 block per stance change).
   - With Violet Lotus (energy on stance exit), this means each stance change gives energy AND block. Amazing synergy.
   - Passive blocking that scales with stance-change frequency.

Both Inner Peace and Mental Fortress are excellent. 

Inner Peace gives: Calm entry (Wrath exit) + draw engine + Violet Lotus energy trigger.
Mental Fortress gives: Passive block on every stance change.

I think Mental Fortress is the pick here. It synergizes perfectly with Violet Lotus - every stance change now gives both +1 energy AND +4 block. This makes the stance-change engine both an energy engine and a defense engine. It's a power so it only needs to be played once.

Actually, Inner Peace is also critical - it IS the stance-change engine. Without cards that enter Calm, I only have Vigilance (2E). Inner Peace is a 1E Calm entry that ALSO draws 3 cards when already in Calm.

Hmm. Mental Fortress is the long-term scaling card. Inner Peace is the engine card. With just the starter deck, I have Eruption (Wrath entry) and Vigilance (Calm entry). Adding Inner Peace gives me a second, cheaper Calm entry. But Mental Fortress converts every stance change into block.

I will take Mental Fortress. My reasoning: it is a power that permanently improves every stance change. Violet Lotus already gives energy per stance exit; Mental Fortress adds block per stance change. Together they make the stance-change loop both energy-positive and block-generating. I can pick up more stance-change cards later.

Hand: Strike, Strike, Vigilance, Defend, Mental Fortress. 3 energy.

I got a Slimed card in my discard (from the Acid Slime debuff). 

Mental Fortress is a POWER - I should play it ASAP so it works for the rest of combat.
Mental Fortress (1E) + gives 4 block on each stance change.

Options:
A) Mental Fortress (1E) + Strike 0 (1E) + Defend (1E) = 3E. Play MF, kill small slime (6 dmg on 7 HP... no, 6 < 7, doesn't kill), 5 block. Total block: 5 from Defend + potentially 0 from MF (no stance change yet). Take 15-5=10 damage.

B) Mental Fortress (1E) + Vigilance (2E) = 3E. MF played, enter Calm (+4 block from MF stance change), +8 block from Vigilance = 12 block. Take 15-12=3 damage. Decent.
